summary: Solid implementation of deterministic tool registry and execution gateway. Schema validation, idempotency replay, and handler registration patterns are well-designed. Integration with orchestration service is clean. 3 medium and 3 low findings — all improvement suggestions, no blockers.

findings.title: Unbounded idempotency caches will grow indefinitely
findings.detail: _seen_invocation_ids and _idempotency_cache accumulate entries for every unique invocation_id but never evict. In a long session with many AI tool calls, memory grows without bound. Recommend adding a max-entries cap (e.g., 1000) with LRU or FIFO eviction, or periodic flush on session boundaries.
findings.recommendation: Add a _max_cache_entries constant and evict oldest entries when exceeded.

findings.title: Schema-driven requires_approval flag computed but not consulted in impact assessment
findings.detail: validate_and_apply correctly sets invocation.requires_approval from schema metadata (e.g., asset_import -> requires_parent_approval: true, logic_edit -> true). However, _assess_impact uses hardcoded tool name lists instead of checking the flag. A parent calling asset_import with ≤5 invocations gets LOW impact despite the schema declaring it requires approval. This creates a gap between declared policy and enforcement.
findings.recommendation: Have _assess_impact check invocation.requires_approval as a first pass — if any invocation has requires_approval=true, return HIGH regardless of actor or count heuristics.

findings.title: safety_check schema has no required arguments
findings.detail: safety_check declares argument_types for text, image_ref, and mode, but required_arguments is empty and allow_unknown_arguments is false. An empty invocation safety_check({}) passes validation, which is semantically meaningless — a safety check with nothing to check.
findings.recommendation: Either add required_arguments: ['text'] or at minimum document that callers must provide at least one of text/image_ref. An alternative is a custom validation rule on the schema.

findings.title: Default execute handler returns full execution context in result
findings.detail: _default_execute includes 'context': context.duplicate(true) in its return value. When context contains actor_id or other sensitive data, and results are cached in _idempotency_cache, this data persists in memory. If results are ever serialized or logged, it becomes a data exposure vector.
findings.recommendation: Omit context from the default execute result, or strip sensitive keys before caching.

findings.title: _stable_serialize returns identical string for all unsupported types
findings.detail: All unrecognized types serialize to '<unsupported>', creating theoretical fingerprint collisions. Mitigated by schema validation rejecting non-deterministic values upstream, but defense-in-depth suggests including type info (e.g., '<unsupported:TYPE_OBJECT>').
findings.recommendation: Change line 188 to return '<unsupported:%d>' % typeof(value) for type-distinct fingerprints.

findings.title: Missing test coverage for register_handler with unknown tool name
findings.detail: Gateway correctly rejects handler registration for tools not in the registry (lines 30-32 in gateway), returning false and logging an error. This path has no contract test coverage.
findings.recommendation: Add an assertion: gateway.register_handler('nonexistent_tool', handler) returns false.

collateral_fix_review.detail: The setup('') change correctly skips file loading when an empty path is passed, while setup() with default parameter still loads the rules file. This is a sensible fix for test isolation — contracts can use defaults-only without depending on filesystem state.

positive_observations: Clean separation of concerns: registry handles schema validation, gateway handles execution + idempotency
Deterministic value checking is thorough — recursive validation of Arrays and Dictionaries
Stable serialization covers Vector2, Vector3, nested structures with sorted dictionary keys
Idempotency replay with fingerprint comparison prevents stale data corruption
Per-tool handler registration with fallback defaults is a flexible extension point
DeterministicToolExecutionGateway properly extends ToolExecutionGateway as a subtype replacement
Registry defaults cover all 5 required tools plus 3 compatibility schemas
31 new test assertions across 2 contract tests with good edge case coverage
